Yidongfang Si

Yidongfang Si  司懿东方  

y_si@mit.edu  |  Curriculum Vitae


Welcome! I'm a Postdoctoral Fellow at MIT, working with Prof. Raffaele Ferrari. I study bottom boundary layer turbulence using theories and numerical models. My research interests lie broadly in geophysical fluid dynamics, turbulence, ice-ocean interactions, eddies, and tides on Earth and other ocean worlds. 

Before joining MIT, I completed my PhD under the guidance of Prof. Andrew Stewart at UCLA. There, I investigated the dynamics of ocean circulation around the Antarctic margins using a combination of theories, numerical simulations, and in-situ data. 



Employment:

  • Jun 2023 – Jun 2025, Houghton Postdoctoral Fellow, Massachusetts Institute of Technology

Education:

    • Sep 2018 – Jun 2023, Ph.D. in Physical Oceanography, University of California, Los Angeles 

    • Sep 2014 – Jul 2018, B.S. in Astronomy, School of Physics, Peking University
